message:
perf(fcp-store): read-lock fast path for symbol-store lookups (br-s5u65)

Closes flywheel_connectors-s5u65 (P2).

Reviewer flagged that MemorySymbolStore::get_symbol, get_all_symbols,
and symbol_count all acquired objects.write() just to inline-scrub
corrupt symbols on every read. Under concurrent lookup load the write-
lock serialized all three read methods plus unrelated writes, so
aggregate throughput collapsed as lookup concurrency rose even when
no corruption was present.

Fix: read-first + write-on-corruption.

- get_symbol: fast path takes objects.read(). If the symbol at the
  target ESI matches current meta (symbol_matches_meta), clone and
  return. If the symbol is physically present but meta-mismatched,
  fall through to the write-lock slow path that runs the existing
  scrub + used_bytes reconciliation. The slow path is only reached
  on actual corruption — the expected hot case stays read-only.
- get_all_symbols: fast path takes objects.read() and filters by
  symbol_matches_meta during the clone walk. The returned Vec is
  identical to the old write-lock + scrub path's output; physical
  map pruning simply defers to the next write operation.
- symbol_count: fast path takes objects.read() and counts matching
  symbols with .filter().count(). Observable u32 is unchanged.

Physical pruning of corrupt entries deferred to the next write
operation (put_symbol, put_object_meta, delete_object) which already
hold the write lock. This is safe because:
  * symbol_matches_meta is idempotent and cheap.
  * used_bytes is already eventually-consistent under concurrent
    puts so letting it lag briefly on corrupt entries is in-budget.
  * Corruption-triggered scrub still happens when get_symbol's slow
    path fires at the exact ESI where the drift occurred.

Regression tests:
- get_symbol_read_path_is_concurrent_safe: 32 OS threads each do 8
  get_symbol + 1 symbol_count calls on the same object; asserts all
  readers agree on the symbol count (==8). Under the old code this
  would have serialized via the global write lock; under the fix
  readers run truly in parallel.
- get_symbol_fast_path_errors_preserve_semantics: pins ObjectNotFound
  and NotFound error shapes on the read-lock path so a future
  refactor can't silently change the API contract.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-store — 805 pass (803 + 2 new regression),
same 2 pre-existing flaky quarantine::evict_expired* failures
(unchanged from ywpup/fm746 baselines).

Cod_4 / SunnyMoose
